package.name: apphub
package.summary: App Hub — desktop host + App Store for apps that connect via the runtime bridge.

doc_split.publisher: Usage only — bridge API, permissions, launch. No package, production, or security architecture.
doc_split.agent_internal: When writing publisher docs, never mention production, host_dev, or package install.

end_user.summary: Uses the App Hub desktop, App Store, and installed apps.

publisher.summary: Build an app that runs in App Hub and uses the bridge to request permissions, read user info, and send desktop messages.
publisher.human_doc: App Hub Guide → App bridge tab (friendly summary; this JSON is the machine-readable contract for AI tools)

hosted.summary: Zip bundle on App Hub. Hub serves …/apps/{slug}/runtime/ in an opaque sandbox (no allow-same-origin).
hosted.manifest: runtime_type: hosted, main or index.html, zip via POST /apps/register
hosted.storage: Hub injects a localStorage shim automatically. Await window.__APPHUB_STORAGE__.ready before first reads. Data is per Hub user + app slug.
hosted.choose_when: Small tools, demos, no publisher HTTPS host

iframe.summary: Publisher hosts a SPA at entry_url. Hub embeds it with allow-same-origin on your origin only.
iframe.manifest: runtime_type: iframe, entry_url (HTTPS), optional healthcheck_url; manifest drop or POST /apps/register JSON
iframe.storage: Normal localStorage/sessionStorage on your publisher origin
iframe.choose_when: Full SPAs, auth, existing domain you control

hosted_storage.automatic: Hub injects hosted-storage-shim.js when serving hosted HTML — publishers use normal localStorage calls.
hosted_storage.ready: await window.__APPHUB_STORAGE__?.ready before localStorage reads on initial load
hosted_storage.scope: Isolated per Hub user and app slug — not shared across hosted apps
hosted_storage.iframe_note: Does not apply to runtime_type iframe — use your own origin storage

hosted_runtime_troubleshooting.title: Hosted runtime — publisher troubleshooting
hosted_runtime_troubleshooting.summary: Common errors when opening a hosted zip in App Hub: zip layout, ES modules (type="module"), CSP frame-ancestors when Hub is iframe-embedded, and runtime serve behavior.

zip_contract.summary: Exact layout for POST /apps/register with multipart field bundle.
layout: manifest.json must be inside the zip (root or one subfolder — Hub picks the shallowest path). Register does not accept a separate manifest for hosted zip uploads.
Entry file: manifest main, bundle_entry, or entry — default index.html. That path must exist in the extracted bundle.
Subfolders (assets/, static/, fonts/) are allowed. Zip only build output (e.g. dist/), never node_modules or dev shims (.cmd, .ps1).
Max size 50 MB (env APPHUB_BUNDLE_MAX_BYTES default 52_428_800; upload rule max 51200 KB).
Blocked file extensions: php, phtml, phar, exe, bat, cmd, sh, ps1, dll, so, dylib.
zip_contract.reference: Minimum valid layout matches example_tree: manifest.json, index.html, and built assets (e.g. assets/*.js) at zip root. Works with Vite ESM (type="module") or plain classic scripts when paths are relative.

javascript_modules.summary: ES modules are supported. Filenames like app-o2KUBx4V.js or index-BmAhrHFo.js come from your bundler (Vite), not from Hub renaming or injecting a loader.
hub_html_changes: Hub does not inject app-{id}.js or remove type="module".
Rewrites every src= and href= in HTML to append launch_token query param (including Vite ./assets/*.js with crossorigin).
Injects one inline classic script id="apphub-hosted-storage-shim" immediately after <head> (localStorage proxy).
Non-HTML files (.js, .css, images) are served unchanged with correct MIME type.
javascript_modules.asset_auth: First request: launch_token on index.html URL. Hub sets HttpOnly cookie apphub_rt_{slug} for follow-up requests. Chunk script tags also get launch_token on src because HTML is rewritten. Missing token → 401 on assets.

import_outside_module.symptom: Uncaught SyntaxError: Cannot use import statement outside a module
likely_causes: A .js URL returns 401, 404, or HTML — browser executes the error body as a classic script.
index.html loads the bundle without type="module".
Zip contains source or wrong folder (not dist/).
publisher_checks: DevTools Elements: served index.html still has <script type="module" …>.
DevTools Network: every .js is 200 with Content-Type application/javascript.
Compare your zip tree to example_tree (manifest.json, index.html, assets/ at zip root).
Optional test: Vite IIFE build (no import/export) — if that works, fix ESM entry wiring in index.html; Hub supports type="module".
import_outside_module.platform_action: None for this error — not CSP frame-ancestors.

csp_framing.summary: Hosted files are served from the Hub API origin inside a sandboxed runner iframe. Responses include CSP frame-ancestors listing who may embed the runtime document.
csp_framing.nested_chain: product shell → Hub SPA iframe → GET …/apps/{slug}/runtime/index.html (API origin document)
csp_framing.headers: Content-Security-Policy on every GET …/runtime/{path} (HTML and assets). frame-ancestors lists allowed parent origins.
frame_ancestors_sources: APPHUB_ALLOWED_HUB_ORIGINS — Hub SPA that embeds the runner
APPHUB_ALLOWED_PRODUCT_ORIGINS — product page that embeds Hub in an iframe

hub_origin and product_origin query params added by Hub frontend after POST …/launch (must already match APPHUB_ALLOWED_HUB_ORIGINS or APPHUB_ALLOWED_PRODUCT_ORIGINS — query params alone do not bypass the allowlist; loopback allowed in local dev)
Referer origin when it matches hub/product allowlists
csp_framing.hub_launch_url: After POST …/launch, Hub appends hub_origin (Hub window origin) and product_origin (product shell embedding Hub) as query params on the hosted runtime URL.
csp_framing.self_only_error: Console: Framing 'https://…' violates frame-ancestors 'self' — Hub or product origin missing from server allowlist. Publishers cannot fix this in the zip.

platform_env.local_dev: APP_ENV=local|testing defaults often include localhost:5173 (Hub) and localhost:3000 (product). Custom Laragon ports need explicit env values.

csp_framing.publisher_action: Send Hub URL and product URL to your platform team. CSP is host configuration, not manifest fields.

runtime_serving.summary: GET /apps/{slug}/runtime/{path} — public route, launch_token auth, serves pinned bundle version from launch.
behaviors: HTML: rewrite src/href launch_token, inject shim + scrollbar CSS, set CSP + cookie.
Assets: BinaryFileResponse, same CSP frame-ancestors, cookie auth when query token absent.
connect-src CSP includes manifest api_urls origins for fetch/XHR to publisher backends.
CORS: reflects hub/product origins for Vite crossorigin asset loads.
runtime_serving.devtools_expectation: View served index.html source (not zip on disk): script src includes launch_token; shim script present; frame-ancestors in response headers.

troubleshooting_checklist.symptom: Cannot use import statement outside a module
troubleshooting_checklist.likely_cause: Chunk loaded as classic script or non-JS response for .js URL
troubleshooting_checklist.publisher_action: Check type="module", Network 200 on all chunks, zip dist/ only

troubleshooting_checklist.likely_cause: launch_token missing on chunk URL; cookie not sent in opaque sandbox
troubleshooting_checklist.publisher_action: Use relative ./assets paths; rely on HTML rewrite (re-publish if index paths wrong)
troubleshooting_checklist.platform_action: Confirm serve rewrite; APPHUB_LAUNCH_TOKEN_TTL not expired mid-load

troubleshooting_checklist.likely_cause: Hub/product origin not in APPHUB_ALLOWED_* env
troubleshooting_checklist.publisher_action: Report framing error + URLs to platform team
troubleshooting_checklist.platform_action: Set APPHUB_ALLOWED_HUB_ORIGINS and APPHUB_ALLOWED_PRODUCT_ORIGINS; verify hub_origin on launch URL

troubleshooting_checklist.publisher_action: Zip only build output (e.g. dist/), not project root or node_modules

troubleshooting_checklist.likely_cause: Silent CSP frame block or wrong bundle entry
troubleshooting_checklist.publisher_action: Check console for CSP; verify manifest main points to existing file
troubleshooting_checklist.platform_action: Fix frame-ancestors allowlist; verify bundle entry file exists on server

build_tools.vite: Default Vite 5/6 dist with type="module" and ./assets/* is the recommended path. base: './' if needed. Zip dist contents + manifest.json at zip root.
build_tools.vite_iife_test: Optional: rollup output format iife to confirm ESM vs classic-script wiring — not required for production.
build_tools.webpack: Relative publicPath (auto or ./) so chunks resolve under …/runtime/.
build_tools.plain_html: Classic <script src> without import/export — no bundler required; same zip layout as example_tree.

bridge.overview: When a user opens your app from the desktop, App Hub loads your runtime in a window and provides AppHubBridge for permissions and desktop features.
connection_flow: User opens your app (desktop icon or App Store).
App Hub resolves launch: POST /apps/{slug}/launch → entry_url + launch_token + scopes_granted (from server consent).
Your app loads in a Hub window; AppHubBridge becomes available (or postMessage channel apphub:bridge).
Use display_user from bridge:ready for UI; call publisher backend GET bridge/user for authoritative identity.

runtime_context.launch_token: Short-lived secret minted at launch. Treat like a password — never log, expose in UI, or send to third parties. Publisher backend passes it to GET bridge/user. scopes_granted on token mirror server install consent only.
runtime_context.bridge_api_base: App Hub API base URL for bridge HTTP routes (from Hub install).
runtime_context.publisher_api_base: First manifest api_urls entry — convenience for calling your tool backend.
runtime_context.hub_locale: Hub UI language code (e.g. en, vi) — default for app copy; apps may still offer their own locale picker.
runtime_context.color_scheme: Hub theme preference: dark | light | auto (follow system when auto).
runtime_context.display_user: UI-only { id, name } from Hub GET /bootstrap — included in apphub:bridge:ready. Not cryptographically verified inside the iframe; use GET bridge/user from your publisher backend for authoritative identity.

discovery.in_window: window.AppHubBridge (provided by docs/sdk-stub.js in sandboxed iframe)
discovery.sdk_stub: docs/sdk-stub.js — copy into your app bundle or load via script tag

context.scopes_granted: string[] — server minted at launch (install consent)
context.session_granted: string[] optional — Hub session desktop consents only (not authoritative)
context.permissions: string[] — manifest permissions for Hub UI
context.launch_token: string|null — short-lived; use via publisher backend only
context.caller_origin: string|null — publisher entry origin when iframe
context.display_user: { id: number, name: string } optional — UI only from Hub bootstrap

permissions.description: Extra profile fields (avatar, locale, etc.) when the user agrees.
Install consent for POST bridge/notify from publisher backend (manifest api_urls). Not a postMessage bridge method.
Show a banner or message on the desktop work area.
permissions.user_prompt: Allow {app} to show messages on your desktop?

permissions.description: Show a count or dot on your app icon in the taskbar.
permissions.user_prompt: Allow {app} to update its taskbar badge?

permissions.description: Save exported files from hosted apps (opaque sandbox blocks blob downloads without this). Enables allow-downloads sandbox and AppHubBridge.saveFile().
permissions.user_prompt: Allow {app} to save files to your device?

manifest.entry_url: string — required for iframe (HTTPS publisher SPA URL); omitted for hosted zip
manifest.healthcheck_url: string optional — iframe draft preflight ping target
manifest.icon: string optional — emoji/short label (max 32) OR bundle-relative image path (icon.png, assets/icon.svg). Hub extracts image files at register and serves GET /apps/{slug}/icon (no launch token). HTTPS icon URL supported for iframe manifests. Optional multipart icon=@file on hosted POST /apps/register.
manifest.permissions: string[] — bridge scopes (user.read, desktop.notify, desktop.download, …). Shown on install and DEV review; Hub UI only. Server records consent on POST /apps/{slug}/bridge-consents from manifest (never from client scope list).
manifest.api_urls: string[] optional — publisher tool backend URL(s) for bridge HTTP. Caller TCP IP must match DNS-pinned IPs at publish (production) or resolve live DNS (local dev). Optional APPHUB_BRIDGE_PROXY_SECRET for loopback port hardening. localhost rejected on publish in production.
manifest.api_base_url: string optional — legacy single URL; merged into api_urls.

display_only.use_for: Greetings, labels, UI copy inside the app iframe
display_only.do_not_use_for: Authorization, audit, or calling your publisher API as this user

authoritative.source: GET bridge/user (launch token + user.read) or POST verify-launch-token on publisher server
authoritative.requires: user.read or user.profile scope on launch token

getDisplayUser.description: UI-only user snapshot from bridge:ready context (Hub bootstrap). No HTTP call. Not for auth.

requestPermission.description: Hub UI consent for desktop.message and desktop.badge during the session. user.* and desktop.notify are granted at install — not via runtime bridge calls.

sendDesktopMessage.description: Show a banner or overlay on the desktop.

setTaskbarBadge.description: Set or clear taskbar badge on your app button.

saveFile.signature: saveFile({ filename, mime, data }): Promise<{ saved: boolean, filename: string }>
saveFile.description: Trigger a browser download from the Hub parent (hosted opaque sandbox). data: base64 string or ArrayBuffer.

reportError.signature: reportError(error: Error | string | { message?: string, name?: string, stack?: string }): Promise<void>
reportError.description: Log a runtime error to App Hub usage logs (action error). Hub shell calls POST /apps/{slug}/usage — no extra permission.

bridge.example:
// After apphub:bridge:ready
const bridge = window.AppHubBridge
const display = bridge.getDisplayUser() // UI greeting — no API
console.log('Hello', display?.name)
// Authoritative user: GET bridge/user from your publisher backend (see http_companion)
await bridge.requestPermission('desktop.message')
await bridge.sendDesktopMessage({ type: 'banner', title: 'Hello', body: `Welcome ${display?.name}`, duration_ms: 5000 })

http_companion.note: Publisher tool backends (manifest api_urls) may call GET bridge/user and POST bridge/notify. App Hub matches caller TCP IP to api_urls. Implement fetch in your app against your api_urls proxy — not Hub postMessage. Hub postMessage is for desktop UI (message, badge) and reportError only.
routes: GET bridge/user — scoped user snapshot (requires user.read on launch token)
POST bridge/notify — notify launching user by default; set broadcast: true or user_ids for wider fan-out (requires desktop.notify on launch token)

deploy.hosted: Build dist/ → zip with manifest.json (runtime_type: hosted) → drop on Hub desktop → POST /apps/register (draft) → DEV approves → active in store.
deploy.local: Drop a local manifest on the desktop for private install (no server listing).
deploy.iframe: Self-hosted entry_url — drop manifest.json (runtime_type: iframe) on Hub desktop or POST /apps/register JSON (draft) → DEV approves → active in store.

steps: Choose runtime_type: hosted (zip on Hub) or iframe (your entry_url SPA).
Open App Hub Guide → App bridge, or read audiences.publisher.bridge here.
Hosted zip issues (ES modules, CSP framing): see audiences.publisher.hosted_runtime_troubleshooting.
Listen for apphub:bridge:ready in your app.
For hosted: await window.__APPHUB_STORAGE__?.ready before localStorage reads on load.
Call requestPermission for desktop scopes as needed.
Use sendDesktopMessage, setTaskbarBadge, saveFile (desktop.download) when granted.
For notifications: POST bridge/notify from your publisher backend (see http_companion).
Handle denied permissions gracefully in your UI.

vi.summary: Xây app chạy trong App Hub, dùng bridge để xin quyền, đọc thông tin user và gửi tin lên desktop.

hosted.summary: Gói zip trên App Hub. Hub phục vụ …/apps/{slug}/runtime/ trong sandbox opaque (không allow-same-origin).
hosted.manifest: runtime_type: hosted, main hoặc index.html, zip qua POST /apps/register
hosted.storage: Hub tự inject shim localStorage. Chờ window.__APPHUB_STORAGE__.ready trước lần đọc đầu. Dữ liệu theo user Hub + slug app.
hosted.choose_when: Tool nhỏ, demo, không có host HTTPS của publisher

iframe.summary: Publisher host SPA tại entry_url. Hub nhúng với allow-same-origin chỉ trên origin của bạn.
iframe.manifest: runtime_type: iframe, entry_url (HTTPS), healthcheck_url tùy chọn; thả manifest hoặc POST /apps/register JSON
iframe.storage: localStorage/sessionStorage bình thường trên origin publisher
iframe.choose_when: SPA đầy đủ, auth, domain bạn kiểm soát

hosted_storage.automatic: Hub inject hosted-storage-shim.js khi phục vụ HTML hosted — publisher gọi localStorage như bình thường.
hosted_storage.ready: await window.__APPHUB_STORAGE__?.ready trước khi đọc localStorage lần đầu khi load
hosted_storage.scope: Tách theo user Hub và slug app — không chia sẻ giữa các app hosted
hosted_storage.iframe_note: Không áp dụng runtime_type iframe — dùng storage trên origin của bạn

hosted_runtime_troubleshooting.title: Hosted runtime — xử lý sự cố publisher
hosted_runtime_troubleshooting.summary: Lỗi thường gặp khi mở zip hosted: bố cục zip, ES module (type="module"), CSP frame-ancestors khi Hub nằm trong iframe sản phẩm.

csp_framing.self_only_error: Console: vi phạm frame-ancestors 'self' — origin Hub hoặc sản phẩm chưa có trong allowlist server. Publisher không sửa được trong zip.
csp_framing.publisher_action: Gửi URL Hub và URL sản phẩm cho đội nền tảng. CSP do host cấu hình.

javascript_modules.summary: Hỗ trợ ES module. Tên file dạng app-xxxxx.js là hash của Vite/bundler, không phải loader do Hub inject.

bridge.overview: Khi user mở app từ desktop, App Hub tải runtime trong cửa sổ và cung cấp AppHubBridge cho quyền và tính năng desktop.
connection_flow: User mở app (icon desktop hoặc App Store).
App Hub launch: POST /apps/{slug}/launch → entry_url + launch_token + scopes_granted (đồng ý trên server).
App tải trong cửa sổ Hub; AppHubBridge sẵn sàng (hoặc kênh postMessage apphub:bridge).
Dùng display_user từ bridge:ready cho UI; backend publisher gọi GET bridge/user cho danh tính tin cậy.

permissions.description: Id user và tên hiển thị.
permissions.user_prompt: Cho phép {app} xem tên của bạn?

permissions.description: Trường hồ sơ mở rộng (avatar, locale, v.v.) khi user đồng ý.
permissions.user_prompt: Cho phép {app} đọc hồ sơ của bạn?

permissions.description: Quyền cài đặt cho POST bridge/notify từ backend publisher (api_urls). Không phải phương thức postMessage bridge.
Hiện banner hoặc tin trên vùng desktop.
permissions.user_prompt: Cho phép {app} hiện tin trên desktop?

permissions.description: Hiện số hoặc chấm trên icon app ở taskbar.
permissions.user_prompt: Cho phép {app} cập nhật badge taskbar?

display_only.use_for: Lời chào, nhãn, copy UI trong iframe app
display_only.do_not_use_for: Phân quyền, audit, hoặc gọi API publisher như user này

authoritative.use_for: Danh tính phía server, quyền, tuân thủ
authoritative.requires: user.read hoặc user.profile trên launch token

getDisplayUser.description: Snapshot user chỉ cho UI từ bridge:ready (bootstrap Hub). Không gọi HTTP. Không dùng cho auth.

requestPermission.description: Đồng ý UI Hub cho desktop.message và desktop.badge trong phiên. user.* và desktop.notify được cấp lúc cài đặt — không qua bridge runtime.

sendDesktopMessage.description: Hiện banner hoặc overlay trên desktop.

setTaskbarBadge.description: Đặt hoặc xóa badge taskbar trên nút app của bạn.

bridge.example:
// Sau apphub:bridge:ready
const bridge = window.AppHubBridge
const display = bridge.getDisplayUser() // Lời chào UI — không gọi API
console.log('Xin chào', display?.name)
// User tin cậy: GET bridge/user từ backend publisher (xem http_companion)
await bridge.requestPermission('desktop.message')
await bridge.sendDesktopMessage({ type: 'banner', title: 'Xin chào', body: `Chào ${display?.name}`, duration_ms: 5000 })

deploy.hosted: Build dist/ → zip có manifest.json (runtime_type: hosted) → thả desktop Hub → POST /apps/register (draft) → DEV duyệt → active trên store.
deploy.local: Thả manifest local trên desktop để cài riêng (không liệt kê server).
deploy.iframe: entry_url tự host — thả manifest.json (runtime_type: iframe) trên desktop Hub hoặc POST /apps/register JSON (draft) → DEV duyệt → active trên store.

steps: Chọn runtime_type: hosted (zip trên Hub) hoặc iframe (SPA entry_url của bạn).
Mở Hướng dẫn App Hub → Cầu nối App, hoặc đọc audiences.publisher.bridge ở đây.
Sự cố zip hosted (ES module, CSP framing): xem audiences.publisher.hosted_runtime_troubleshooting.
Hosted: await window.__APPHUB_STORAGE__?.ready trước khi đọc localStorage khi load.
Gọi requestPermission cho quyền desktop khi cần.
Dùng sendDesktopMessage, setTaskbarBadge khi được cấp.
Thông báo desktop: POST bridge/notify từ backend publisher (xem http_companion).
Xử lý khi user từ chối quyền một cách graceful trong UI.

agent.instructions: Publisher docs = usage only (bridge). Security and package details are host_dev / backend_security — never put those in publisher-facing text.

host_dev.summary: Host team embeds App Hub in the product. Owns backend endpoint security.

backend_security.principle: Publisher apps are untrusted USER-tier. Endpoint layer enforces isolation — publishers never see this section.
backend_security.publisher_isolation: Apps run in Hub windows. Only bridge routes and scoped launch_token. No host DB, env, or admin APIs.
endpoint_rules: GET integration-docs/internal requires X-AppHub-Host-Access matching APPHUB_HOST_ACCESS_SECRET (not packages-core zone/server manager).
POST /apps/{slug}/install-intent mints short-lived token when permission dialog opens.
POST /apps/{slug}/bridge-consents records manifest permissions after Accept (requires intent_token).
POST /apps/{slug}/launch mints launch_token with scopes_granted from server consent records only.
Validate launch_token per app slug and session; user_id from auth, not client headers.
Filter bridge/user fields by granted scopes and packages-core zones.
Reject bridge calls without matching scope consent record.

hosted_runtime_framing.summary: Hosted bundles are served from the API and embedded in a Hub runner iframe. When Hub itself is in a product iframe, the browser checks frame-ancestors for every ancestor origin.

laravel_env.APPHUB_ALLOWED_PRODUCT_ORIGINS: Product shell origin(s) that embed Hub in an iframe

hosted_runtime_framing.parent_post_message: Include productOrigin: window.location.origin in apphub:host config message
hosted_runtime_framing.launch_url_query: Hub adds hub_origin and product_origin on hosted runtime URL after POST …/launch
hosted_runtime_framing.local_dev_defaults: Hub http://localhost:5173, product http://localhost:3000 when APP_ENV=local
hosted_runtime_framing.misconfiguration_symptom: launch OK, runtime/index.html OK, blank app window, no CSS/JS requests

iframe_runtime_policy.summary: Per-app apps.entry_url + DEV approval is the allowlist. Optional enterprise cap via APPHUB_ALLOWED_RUNTIME_ORIGINS.
iframe_runtime_policy.production_opt_in: APPHUB_ALLOW_ANY_PUBLISHER_RUNTIME_ORIGIN=true when enterprise list is empty and catalog+DEV model is intended
iframe_runtime_policy.sandbox: iframe runtime uses allow-same-origin on publisher origin only — does not expose Hub localStorage

hosted_storage.summary: Hosted zip apps get localStorage shim injected at serve time; Hub parent persists via apphub:storage postMessage.

routes.description: Mint short-lived launch token and return entry_url. Body optional: { version? }. scopes_granted on token come from server install consent — never from client.
Tool backend verifies launch_token once. Body: { launch_token, app_slug? }. Caller IP must match manifest api_urls host. Returns user_id + scopes.
Log app usage. Body: { action: app_open|error, metadata? }.
Server-side GET to app healthcheck_url. Requires launch permission. Returns { ok, status, latency_ms, error? }.
Publisher-safe integration contract (audiences.publisher + bridge routes only). Public JSON for AI tools and Guide.
Full contract — host integrator only (X-AppHub-Host-Access + APPHUB_HOST_ACCESS_SECRET).
Catalog. Query: mode=store|draft|publisher (default store). publisher = owner draft+active. cursor?, per_page?. meta: has_more, next_cursor.
Hub session bootstrap.
Mint short-lived install intent when permission dialog opens. Body optional: { version? }.
Record install consent after Accept. Body: { intent_token, version? }. Server writes manifest permissions — client must not send scope list.
Scoped user info. Headers: X-AppHub-Launch-Token, X-AppHub-App-Slug.
Notify installed users with desktop.notify consent. Defaults to launching user only; broadcast: true or user_ids for wider fan-out (capped). Caller IP must match manifest api_urls.
All apps including disabled — APPHUB_DEV_USER_IDS only.
Set app status (draft|active|disabled). Dev users only.
Emergency disable app. Dev users only.
Publish: hosted = multipart bundle zip (manifest.json inside) + optional icon file; iframe = JSON body with runtime_type iframe + entry_url (no zip). Creates or upgrades draft app.
Public app icon image (PNG/SVG/WebP) persisted at register. Catalog returns icon_url. No launch token.
Version history for hosted app (semver, hash, uploaded_at).

Serve hosted bundle files. launch_token query on index; HttpOnly cookie for assets. CSP frame-ancestors allows APPHUB_ALLOWED_HUB_ORIGINS + APPHUB_ALLOWED_PRODUCT_ORIGINS (+ hub_origin/product_origin query from Hub). Required for nested iframe: product → hub → app.
DEV review: file tree + bundle hash for hosted app.
DEV review: read text source file from hosted bundle. Query: path.
